Note

License reminder: this project is distributed under a Custom restricted license (no redistribution). See the repository LICENSE file. Disclaimer: this simulator is provided as-is for research, calibration, and educational use. Users assume all risks, including any direct or indirect damage, data loss, hardware damage, injury, or regulatory non-compliance arising from use or misuse.

src.core.power_model

Power Supply Model

This module provides models of the DC power supply feeding the inverter. A time-dependent voltage profile can be defined to simulate battery sag, AC ripple rectification, or other variations.

author:

BLDC Control Team

version:

0.8.0

Added in version 0.8.0: Initial implementation of power supply models

Functions

compute_efficiency_metrics(input_power_w, ...)

Compute simple drivetrain efficiency metrics from power flow values.

compute_power_metrics(voltage, current[, ...])

Compute basic power-quality metrics from voltage/current waveforms.

recommend_efficiency_adjustments(efficiency, ...)

Return simple heuristic suggestions for improving simulated efficiency.

required_reactive_compensation(...)

Estimate reactive compensation needed to improve power factor.

Classes

ConstantSupply([voltage])

Constant DC voltage supply.

EfficiencyAdjustmentRecommendation

Structured recommendation payload for efficiency heuristics.

PowerFactorController([target_pf, kp, ki, ...])

Simple closed-loop power factor correction command generator.

RampSupply(initial, final, duration)

Linear ramp of supply voltage.

SupplyProfile()

Abstract base class for supply voltage profiles.

VariableSupply([voltage_func, time_points, ...])

Custom time-dependent supply using function or data points.
class src.core.power_model.EfficiencyAdjustmentRecommendation[source]

Bases: TypedDict

Structured recommendation payload for efficiency heuristics.

efficiency: float
power_factor: float
target_efficiency: float
needs_attention: bool
suggestions: list[str]
src.core.power_model.compute_power_metrics(voltage, current, backend='cpu')[source]

Compute basic power-quality metrics from voltage/current waveforms.

Return type:

dict[str, float]

Parameters

voltagenp.ndarray

Instantaneous voltage samples.

currentnp.ndarray

Instantaneous current samples, aligned with voltage.

Returns

dict

Active/apparent/reactive power, RMS values, and power factor.

src.core.power_model.required_reactive_compensation(active_power_w, current_pf, target_pf)[source]

Estimate reactive compensation needed to improve power factor.

Return type:

dict[str, float]

Notes

This helper assumes positive active power and lagging-load correction sizing.

src.core.power_model.compute_efficiency_metrics(input_power_w, torque_nm, omega_rad_s)[source]

Compute simple drivetrain efficiency metrics from power flow values.

Return type:

dict[str, float]

src.core.power_model.recommend_efficiency_adjustments(efficiency, power_factor, device_drop_v=0.0, dead_time_fraction=0.0, conduction_resistance_ohm=0.0, switching_frequency_hz=0.0, switching_loss_coeff_v_per_a_khz=0.0, target_efficiency=0.9)[source]

Return simple heuristic suggestions for improving simulated efficiency.

Return type:

EfficiencyAdjustmentRecommendation

class src.core.power_model.PowerFactorController(target_pf=0.95, kp=0.1, ki=1.0, max_compensation_var=10000.0)[source]

Bases: object

Simple closed-loop power factor correction command generator.

The controller estimates a reactive compensation command from current PF, active power, and a target PF. It is designed as a non-invasive telemetry hook for simulation workflows.

__init__(target_pf=0.95, kp=0.1, ki=1.0, max_compensation_var=10000.0)[source]
reset()[source]
Return type:

None

update(current_pf, active_power_w, dt)[source]

Update controller and return recommended compensation [VAR].

Return type:

float

class src.core.power_model.SupplyProfile[source]

Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for supply voltage profiles.

abstractmethod get_voltage(time)[source]

Return supply voltage at time t.

Return type:

float

reset()[source]

Reset profile state if applicable.

Return type:

None

Constant DC voltage supply.

class src.core.power_model.RampSupply(initial, final, duration)[source]

Bases: SupplyProfile

Linear ramp of supply voltage.

Allows simulating startup charge or sag.

__init__(initial, final, duration)[source]
get_voltage(time)[source]
